summary: Joel provides a step-by-step tutorial for integrating Markdown and SmartyPants text processors with EditPad Pro on Windows. The post walks through installing Perl, downloading the necessary scripts, creating a batch file to chain the two processors together, and configuring EditPad Pro's tools menu to run the pipeline. The end result is a one-click workflow that converts a text file to HTML with proper typography and opens it in a browser. It's a brief, purely practical how-to post with no broader argument or commentary.
key_points: Markdown converts plain text formatting conventions to HTML, while SmartyPants handles typographic niceties like curly quotes
EditPad Pro is recommended as a fast, feature-rich Notepad replacement for Windows users
The two Perl scripts can be chained together via a simple batch file to produce well-formatted HTML output
EditPad Pro's configurable Tools menu allows integrating external scripts into a one-click workflow
money_quotes: EditPad Pro is a very respectable text editor for Windows. It's fast and contains scrillions of useful features.
It's not the fanciest thing in the world, but if you're still using Notepad for the occasional bits of text, it's a fine drop-in replacement.
key_insight: Chaining Markdown and SmartyPants through a batch file integrated into a text editor's tools menu creates a simple, effective plain-text-to-HTML authoring workflow on Windows.
